rxn_class_summary: Electroreductive 1,2-hydroxy fluorosulfonylation of alkenes with FSO2Cl and O2 (air) to deliver beta-hydroxy sulfonyl fluorides via radical chain involving FSO2 radical, peroxy radical, and reduction by B2(OH)4/Et3SiH.
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of FSO2Cl at Zn cathode generates FSO2 radical (and Cl-); the low local FSO2Cl concentration at the cathode suppresses Cl-atom transfer and favors O2 interception of the benzylic radical. Aluminum sacrificial anode supports the circuit.
electron_flow: At the Zn cathode, FSO2Cl is reduced to FSO2• (and Cl-); FSO2• adds to styrene to give benzylic radical I, which is trapped by O2 to peroxy radical II; HAT from Et3SiH gives hydroperoxide III, which is reduced by B2(OH)4 through borate intermediate IV/VI and hydrolyzed to the beta-hydroxy sulfonyl fluoride, while Et3Si• regenerates FSO2• via halogen-atom transfer with FSO2Cl to sustain the chain.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely inhibited, supporting a radical pathway.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE, 58)
probe_reagents.observation: Trapping of FSO2• afforded adduct 59 in 10% yield, supporting FSO2 radical generation.

probe_reagents.name: beta-keto sulfonyl fluoride 4 (resubjection)
probe_reagents.observation: Compound 4 was not reduced to 3 under standard conditions; products 3 and 4 arise via divergent pathways.

probe_reagents.name: constant potential electrolysis
probe_reagents.observation: Optimal at E_cathode = -0.1 V (87%); more reductive potentials (-0.5, -1.0 V) gave lower yields, indicating mild reduction is needed.

probe_reagents.name: N2 atmosphere control
probe_reagents.observation: Under N2, no product formed and styrene was recovered, confirming O2 is essential.

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of Et3SiH and B2(OH)4
probe_reagents.observation: Neither Et3SiH nor B2(OH)4 is directly involved in the electrochemical step (no redox features).

probe_reagents.name: 11B NMR detection of B(OH)3
probe_reagents.observation: B(OH)3 detected, consistent with B2(OH)4 reducing the hydroperoxide via borate intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: HRMS-ESI detection of (Et3Si)2
probe_reagents.observation: Hexaethyldisilane detected, supporting Et3Si• as chain carrier (and competing homocoupling).

notes: Competing pathways outlined in Fig. 5c (right): in the absence of Et3SiH/B2(OH)4, peroxy radical II undergoes Russell fragmentation to give beta-keto sulfonyl fluoride 4 plus 3; an alternative cathodic reduction of an alkoxy radical to alkoxide is also drawn. A separate EDA-complex (styrene·FSO2Cl) pathway under daylight without electricity also gives product (78%) but with limited scope. Constant cell voltage (not constant current) is required to maintain low local [FSO2Cl] and avoid over-reduction.
